problem: Each face of a cube is painted either  red or blue, each with probability 1/2. The color of each face is determined independently.  What is the probability that the painted cube can be placed on a horizontal surface so that the four vertical faces are all the same color?

classified_questions.question: What is the total number of possible arrangements of colors on the faces of the cube?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How many arrangements would have all six faces of the same color?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How many arrangements would have exactly five faces of the same color?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can you explain why each of these cases would allow the cube to be placed so that the four vertical faces are the same color?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Are there any other arrangements that could also satisfy the condition of having the four vertical faces the same color? If so, can you describe them?
classified_questions.label: e

classified_questions.question: Based on the total number of suitable arrangements and the total number of possible arrangements, what is the probability that the painted cube can be placed as described?
classified_questions.label: b
